title: Compucorp autonomous code reviewer output (v1)
description: Structured output from a fresh-context reviewer subagent dispatched per prompts/code-reviewer.md. Symphony's audit (analyze-run.py) and the parent agent's loop policy depend on schema-strict output.

reviewer_prompt_version.description: Echoes prompts/code-reviewer.md frontmatter's prompt_version. Lets analyze-run.py detect cases where an outdated prompt was used.

verdict.description: approve = no BLOCKERs and no unresolved QUESTIONs and (if re-review) all prior findings resolved. reject = otherwise.

summary.description: One- or two-sentence rationale for the verdict, plain English. Goes into PR Comments if the verdict is approve, or into the Jira escalation comment if reject after final round.

trace.description: Optional but recommended: the reviewer's explicit ticket-symptom -> plan-hypothesis -> diff-hunk trace, for audit. If the trace cannot be made, output a BLOCKER finding in `findings` and leave this null.

findings.description: All findings from this review round. Empty array = no issues.

id.description: Stable identifier for this finding within the round (e.g. 'r2-blocker-1'). Used by the next round's prior_findings_addressed entries.

severity.description: Compucorp severity rubric (ai-code-review.md). BLOCKER blocks PR, WARNING should be fixed or documented, SUGGESTION is optional, QUESTION needs human input (escalates).

file.description: Relative path within <workspace>/repo. Use 'general' if not tied to a specific file (e.g. missing test, PR body issue).

line.description: 1-based line in the post-fix file. Null if not applicable.

message.description: What is wrong + why it matters. Cite file:line if available. Be specific.

suggested_fix.description: Concrete fix instruction. Optional but strongly preferred.

prior_findings_addressed.description: Required when prior_findings is passed as input. One entry per prior finding by id. Missing ids = output is incomplete and parent must re-prompt.

id.description: Matches the id field from the prior round's finding.

evidence.description: For resolved: cite file:line of the fix. For still_applies: explain why the parent's attempted fix is insufficient. For superseded: cite the change that obviated the finding.
